# toll-booth-dvm

> Connecting paid HTTP APIs to Nostr's AI marketplace is hard — you need to handle NIP-90 job events, relay Lightning invoices, poll for settlement, and publish results, all without holding funds. toll-booth-dvm is a TypeScript library that bridges any toll-booth-gated API to Nostr as a Data Vending Machine (DVM).

Operators who already gate their APIs with `@forgesworn/toll-booth` can expose them to Nostr clients with two function calls: `announce` (publish a NIP-89 discovery event) and `serve` (start the relay loop). The DVM handles the full L402 payment flow — relaying bolt11 invoices, polling for settlement, retrying with credentials — without ever holding or forwarding funds.

## Install

```
npm install toll-booth-dvm
```

Single runtime dependency: `nostr-tools`.

---

## Quick start

```typescript
import { serve } from 'toll-booth-dvm'

const dvm = await serve({
  secretKey: process.env.NOSTR_SK!,
  relays: ['wss://relay.damus.io', 'wss://nos.lol'],
  endpoint: 'http://localhost:3000',
  announceOnStart: true,
  boothConfig: {
    serviceName: 'My Routing API',
    pricing: { '/route': 10 },
  },
  about: 'Lightning-paid routing API',
})

process.on('SIGINT', () => dvm.close())
```

---

## Full API Reference

### `announce(boothConfig, options): Promise<Announcement>`

Publish a NIP-89 kind 31990 handler event and a kind 31402 paid service announcement. Maps the toll-booth pricing configuration into signed Nostr events and publishes them to the specified relays. The secret key is zeroised after signing.

```typescript
import { announce } from 'toll-booth-dvm'

const result = await announce(
  {
    serviceName: 'My Routing API',
    pricing: {
      '/route': 10,              // 10 sats
      '/isochrone': { sats: 5, usd: 0.01 },
    },
  },
  {
    secretKey: process.env.NOSTR_SK!,
    relays: ['wss://relay.damus.io'],
    urls: ['https://routing.example.com'],
    about: 'Lightning-paid routing API using OpenStreetMap data',
    topics: ['routing', 'maps', 'openstreetmap'],
    identifier: 'my-routing-api',   // NIP-89 d-tag (defaults to slugified serviceName)
    picture: 'https://example.com/icon.png',
    version: '1.0.0',
  }
)

// result.eventId           — kind 31990 event ID
// result.paidServiceEventId — kind 31402 event ID
// result.relays            — relay list the events were published to
```

Parameters — `AnnounceOptions`:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `secretKey` | `string` | Yes | Hex-encoded 32-byte Nostr secret key. Zeroised after signing. |
| `relays` | `string[]` | Yes | Nostr relay URLs to publish to (at least one required) |
| `urls` | `string[]` | Yes | Public URLs where the toll-booth endpoint is reachable |
| `about` | `string` | Yes | Human-readable service description |
| `topics` | `string[]?` | No | Topic tags for discoverability (e.g. `['routing', 'maps']`) |
| `identifier` | `string?` | No | NIP-89 `d` tag — defaults to slugified service name |
| `picture` | `string?` | No | Profile picture URL for the DVM |
| `version` | `string?` | No | Semantic version string |

Returns `Announcement`:

```typescript
interface Announcement {
  eventId: string           // Nostr event ID of the kind 31990 event
  paidServiceEventId: string // Nostr event ID of the kind 31402 event
  relays: string[]          // Relays the events were published to
}
```

---

### `serve(options): Promise<DvmHandle>`

Start the DVM relay loop. Subscribes to kind 5800 job requests tagged with the DVM's pubkey, proxies them to the upstream toll-booth endpoint, handles the full L402 payment flow, and publishes kind 6800 results.

```typescript
import { serve } from 'toll-booth-dvm'

const dvm = await serve({
  secretKey: process.env.NOSTR_SK!,
  relays: ['wss://relay.damus.io', 'wss://nos.lol'],
  endpoint: 'https://routing.example.com',
  about: 'Lightning-paid routing API',
  announceOnStart: true,
  boothConfig: {
    serviceName: 'Routing API',
    pricing: { '/route': 10, '/isochrone': 5 },
  },
  allowedPaths: ['/route', '/isochrone'],
  maxPendingJobs: 10,
  requestTimeoutMs: 30_000,
  paymentTimeoutMs: 300_000,
  pollIntervalMs: 2_000,
  maxBodyBytes: 65_536,
  maxResponseBytes: 1_048_576,
})

// Graceful shutdown
await dvm.close()
```

Parameters — `ServeOptions`:

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `secretKey` | `string` | — | Hex-encoded 32-byte Nostr secret key. Zeroised on `close()`. |
| `relays` | `string[]` | — | Nostr relay URLs to subscribe on |
| `endpoint` | `string` | — | Upstream toll-booth base URL (e.g. `'http://localhost:3000'`) |
| `about` | `string?` | — | Service description used in announcements |
| `announceOnStart` | `boolean?` | `false` | Publish a kind 31990 handler event on startup |
| `boothConfig` | `BoothConfigLike?` | — | Required if `announceOnStart` is true |
| `pollIntervalMs` | `number?` | `2000` | Lightning settlement poll interval in ms |
| `maxPendingJobs` | `number?` | `10` | Max concurrent in-flight jobs |
| `requestTimeoutMs` | `number?` | `30000` | Upstream HTTP request timeout in ms |
| `paymentTimeoutMs` | `number?` | `300000` | Time to wait for Lightning settlement in ms |
| `allowedPaths` | `string[]?` | (all) | Whitelist of permitted request paths |
| `maxBodyBytes` | `number?` | `65536` | Max request body size in bytes |
| `maxResponseBytes` | `number?` | `1048576` | Max upstream response size in bytes (1 MB) |
| `proxy` | `string?` | — | SOCKS5 proxy URL (stub — not yet implemented) |

Returns `DvmHandle`:

```typescript
interface DvmHandle {
  close(): Promise<void>  // Stop relay loop, cancel pending jobs, zeroise secret key
}
```

---

## L402 payment flow

When a client sends a kind 5800 job request:

1. DVM proxies the request to the upstream toll-booth endpoint.
2. If the upstream returns HTTP 200: DVM publishes kind 6800 result immediately.
3. If the upstream returns HTTP 402: DVM publishes a kind 7000 feedback event with `status: payment-required` and the bolt11 invoice.
4. DVM polls the upstream `/invoice-status/{paymentHash}?token={statusToken}` endpoint until settled or timed out.
5. Once settled: DVM retries the request with `L402 {macaroon}:{preimage}` credentials.
6. On success: DVM publishes the kind 6800 result.

The DVM is non-custodial — bolt11 strings are relayed but never stored beyond the request lifecycle.

Optional bid validation: if the client includes a `bid` tag (millisats), the DVM rejects jobs where the required payment exceeds the bid.

---

## Nostr event kinds

```typescript
import { JOB_KIND, RESULT_KIND, FEEDBACK_KIND, HANDLER_KIND, PAID_SERVICES_KIND } from 'toll-booth-dvm'

JOB_KIND          // 5800  — NIP-90 job request
RESULT_KIND       // 6800  — NIP-90 job result
FEEDBACK_KIND     // 7000  — NIP-90 status/payment feedback
HANDLER_KIND      // 31990 — NIP-89 application handler (DVM discovery)
PAID_SERVICES_KIND // 31402 — kind 31402 paid API service announcement
```

---

## Types

### `BoothConfigLike`

Toll-booth pricing configuration. Maps endpoint paths to prices.

```typescript
interface BoothConfigLike {
  pricing: Record<string, number | PriceInfo | Record<string, number | PriceInfo>>
  serviceName?: string
  hasBackend?: boolean
  xcashu?: { mints: string[] }           // Cashu ecash payment support
  ietfPayment?: { realm: string }        // IETF Payment authentication (draft-ryan-httpauth-payment-01)
}
```

Pricing examples:

```typescript
// Simple: flat sats per path
pricing: { '/route': 10 }

// Mixed: sats and USD
pricing: { '/route': { sats: 10, usd: 0.02 } }

// Nested: default plus per-method overrides
pricing: { '/route': { default: 10, POST: 15 } }
```

### `PriceInfo`

```typescript
interface PriceInfo {
  sats?: number
  usd?: number
}
```

### `AnnounceOptions` — see `announce()` above

### `ServeOptions` — see `serve()` above

### `DvmHandle` — see `serve()` above

### `Announcement` — see `announce()` above

---

## Security details

- Secret keys are zeroised after use (`sk.fill(0)`); the hex string cannot be zeroised (JS strings are immutable)
- `FinalizationRegistry` zeroises the key if the `DvmHandle` is GC'd without calling `close()`
- Deduplication via in-memory seen map with 10-minute TTL, capped at 100k entries
- Path validation: decodes percent-encoding before checking for `..` and `//` traversal
- HTTP method validation: only GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE forwarded to upstream
- Event age validation: rejects kind 5800 events with `created_at` more than 10 minutes from now
- Payment hash validated as 64-char hex before polling; `statusToken` URL-encoded in query string

---

## Examples

The `examples/` directory contains ready-to-run demos:

- `examples/local-demo.ts` — full L402 flow with a mock toll-booth server (zero external setup required)
- `examples/announce.ts` — publish a NIP-89 discovery event to relays
- `examples/serve.ts` — start the relay loop against a real toll-booth endpoint

---

## Links

- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/toll-booth-dvm)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/forgesworn/toll-booth-dvm)
- [@forgesworn/toll-booth](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@forgesworn/toll-booth) — the upstream API gating middleware
- [NIP-90](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/90.md) — Data Vending Machines
- [NIP-89](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/89.md) — Application Handlers
